course.title: Build and Ship an Authorized Web Crawler and Accessible Site Auditor in Go 1.26
course.description: Advanced Go backend and web learners ready to integrate authorization, web standards, net/http ownership, bounded goroutine scheduling, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, accessibility auditing, secure reporting, native testing, deployment, and recovery into one cumulative crawler product. This original, research-backed path uses repeated retrieval, cumulative artifacts, changed-case labs, stakeholder projects, and a performance exam.
outcomes: Demonstrate a cumulative crawler and accessible site auditor from explicit owner authorization through url and robots policy, bounded net/http retrieval, goquery extraction, race-safe frontiers, per-origin goroutine scheduling, colly adoption, durable checkpoints, site-quality audits, safe reports, native tests, operations, and recovery through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.

Demonstrate go 1.26.5 source-to-binary proof; rfc 9309; sitemaps; net/url; net/http; net/netip; x/net/html 0.57; goquery 1.12; colly 2.3; robotstxt 1.1; concurrency, security, accessibility, privacy, testing, deployment, and incident evidence through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.

Demonstrate original deterministic browser models plus controlled compiler, httptest, dns, filesystem, parser, race, fuzz, colly, browser service, accessibility, load, binary, container, restore, rollback, legal-review, and production transfer gates through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.
Demonstrate five distinct stakeholder projects and a cumulative unfamiliar-site production defense through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.
sharedRequirements: Every activity retrieves stakeholder outcome, owner permission, revision and Go version, seed and URL key, origin and path policy, robots and sitemap evidence, request and response budgets, parser and record provenance, frontier and goroutine ownership, privacy and accessibility requirement, failure, cleanup, recovery, and controlled transfer limit before adding one boundary.

Browser Go uses original fixed fixtures and pure bounded models only. It does not open sockets, resolve DNS, import native parser or crawler packages, read host files, retain credentials, launch processes or unbounded goroutines, or claim live accessibility, privacy, legal, race, load, binary, container, restore, or production behavior.

Passing work requires prediction, exact intermediate evidence, compile-ready accepted and rejected cases, a changed case, a test that catches a deliberate defect, accessible stakeholder output, count reconciliation, and explicit ownership of remaining risk.

credential.title: Build and Ship an Authorized Web Crawler and Accessible Site Auditor in Go 1.26 Certificate

modules.title: Owner Duty, Stakeholder Outcomes, Privacy, and Evidence
modules.description: A team proposes a fast crawler because pages are public but cannot name the site owner, affected users, permitted purpose, stop contact, retained fields, or useful decision. Learners cumulatively produce owner-approved site-quality mission charter through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Define stakeholder, affected users, decision, page population, acceptable delay, success, failure harm, and observable value.
Record controlling owner, allowed product identity, origins, paths, methods, schedule, purpose, expiry, and revocation contact.
Separate robots preferences and technical access from contracts, privacy, copyright, jurisdiction, and qualified legal review.

modules.title: Go 1.26 Toolchain, Module Graph, Dependencies, and Reproduction
modules.description: The binary comes from an unknown revision with floating modules, undocumented build tags, warm caches, unreviewed transitive code, and no clean rebuild. Learners cumulatively produce reproducible Go crawler repository baseline through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Pin Go 1.26.5, platform, architecture, module mode, build tags, toolchain directive, and supported runtime matrix.
Pin goquery 1.12.0, Colly 2.3.0, robotstxt 1.1.2, and x/net 0.57.0 with compatibility, provenance, license, and maintenance evidence.
Bind source revision, module graph, generated fixtures, embed inputs, build settings, VCS metadata, binary digest, and configuration schema.

modules.title: Package Boundaries, Interfaces, Value Ownership, and Lifecycle
modules.description: Callbacks mutate shared maps, packages import concrete clients and global loggers, byte slices outlive response bodies, and shutdown cannot account for goroutines. Learners cumulatively produce directed owned Go crawler graph through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Direct policy, fetch, robots, parse, extract, frontier, audit, store, report, and command dependencies inward through consumer-owned interfaces.
Specify ownership and copy rules for URLs, headers, byte slices, DOM selections, records, errors, and report rows crossing boundaries.
Define minimal interfaces at use sites and retain concrete constructors and lifecycle ownership at composition boundaries.

modules.title: Seeds, Origins, Paths, Budgets, and Kill Policy
modules.description: One starting URL expands to subdomains, calendar archives, query permutations, media files, logout actions, and endless retries with no explicit resource ceiling. Learners cumulatively produce testable bounded crawl charter through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Bind each seed to owner authorization, purpose, expected page class, policy revision, contact, start window, and expiry.
Define allowed schemes, ASCII hosts, ports, subdomains, redirects, and embedded resources under deny-by-default policy.
Express methods, path prefixes, queries, fragments, pagination, downloads, forms, and content types as deterministic admission rules.

modules.title: net/url References, Escaping, Host Identity, and Conservative Keys
modules.description: Opaque and hierarchical URLs, RawPath, default ports, fragments, Unicode hosts, query encoding, and base resolution are reduced to unsafe string operations. Learners cumulatively produce Go URL identity and scope admission pipeline through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Use net/url parsing while distinguishing scheme, opaque data, userinfo, host, port, Path, RawPath, RawQuery, and Fragment.
Resolve reference forms against final response and valid document base URLs while retaining raw and resolved provenance.
Compare decoded and escaped paths deliberately and reject ambiguous, invalid, double-encoded, or policy-changing representations.

modules.title: RFC 9309 Matching, Failure Semantics, Caching, and Go Adapter
modules.description: The crawler trusts a Go package name, matches the first rule, fetches robots from the wrong service, ignores encoded paths, and treats an outage as permission. Learners cumulatively produce conformance-tested robots decision engine through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Fetch `/robots.txt` at the correct service origin with an honest stable product token and contact identity.
Apply RFC 9309 product-token matching, group merge, percent encoding, longest rule, and allow tie behavior.
Classify success, redirects, unavailable, unreachable, oversized, malformed, stale cache, and owner-stop states conservatively.

modules.title: Streaming Sitemap XML, Index Graphs, and Admission Bounds
modules.description: The program unmarshals entire sitemap indexes, accepts foreign locations, follows nested cycles, trusts lastmod, and loses the index path that discovered each URL. Learners cumulatively produce streaming bounded sitemap graph through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Use encoding/xml tokens to validate namespace, root, UTF-8, loc, escaping, depth, counts, bytes, and trailing content without whole-tree allocation.
Model sitemap indexes as a bounded directed graph with unique identities, depth limits, cycle detection, and stable traversal.
Apply protocol location rules plus authorization, URL, robots, redirect, DNS, and SSRF policy to every sitemap and page entry.

modules.title: net/http Transport Ownership, Redirects, Bodies, and Timeouts
modules.description: Every request creates a Client, redirects escape policy, response bodies leak on errors, Client.Timeout hides phase causes, and ReadAll ignores size. Learners cumulatively produce owned bounded net/http retrieval adapter through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Configure and reuse one owned Transport with explicit proxy, dial, TLS, idle pool, compression, limits, and CloseIdleConnections policy.
Build safe retrieval requests with context, honest User-Agent and contact, Accept, validators, method, credentials, and trace identity.
Use CheckRedirect to reapply authorization, URL, credentials, method, DNS, loop, and hop budgets while retaining the chain.

modules.title: DNS Resolution, Address Classes, Redirects, Proxies, and SSRF
modules.description: A host passes validation before DNS then resolves or redirects to loopback, private, link-local, metadata, IPv4-mapped, zone-scoped, or rebinding targets. Learners cumulatively produce validated connection-time dial policy through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Allowlist HTTP schemes and ports, reject userinfo and ambiguous host syntax, and prohibit unsupported protocols.
Use net/netip to classify all IPv4 and IPv6 answers including mapped, loopback, private, link-local, multicast, unspecified, and metadata ranges.
Bind admitted addresses to DialContext or a controlled egress proxy and verify each new connection and re-resolution.

modules.title: Content Codings, Character Sets, Media Admission, and Digests
modules.description: Compressed and decoded sizes are conflated, unknown encodings are guessed, binary responses reach HTML parsing, and one digest represents every stage. Learners cumulatively produce representation decoding and identity ledger through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Track wire, compressed, decoded, retained, parsed, and extracted sizes and stop before resource ceilings.
Validate content-coding order and classify unsupported, corrupt, partial, over-budget, and cancellation outcomes.
Use transport media type plus conservative sniff evidence to accept HTML or XML and reject binary and ambiguous bodies.

modules.title: HTML Tokenization, Tree Construction, goquery, and Parser Differentials
modules.description: Source markup is treated as browser DOM, tokenizer and tree APIs are mixed, goquery is assumed to run JavaScript, malformed HTML repair is never tested. Learners cumulatively produce budgeted Go HTML tree adapter through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Choose streaming tokenizer or x/net/html tree from required context, memory, malformed-input behavior, and selector needs.
Construct goquery documents from admitted readers and preserve base, source, parser, and response identity outside selections.
Test implied elements, misnesting, tables, templates, foreign content, comments, duplicate attributes, and deep input.

modules.title: Selectors, Cardinality, Domain Types, and Extraction Drift
modules.description: A goquery Find call takes the first match, strings flow directly into records, absent and empty collapse, and selector drift silently changes meaning. Learners cumulatively produce typed cardinality-aware extraction pipeline through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Define selection root, selector support, expected cardinality, fallback, exclusion, and source evidence for each field.
Return explicit zero, one, many, invalid, conflicting, and fallback outcomes rather than hiding them behind Find and First.
Parse external strings through constructors for URLs, language, dates, identifiers, enumerations, and bounded text.

modules.title: Versioned Records, Field Provenance, Reconciliation, and Corrections
modules.description: The tool emits structs without source spans, parser versions, transformations, rejected values, or lineage and cannot explain or correct a field. Learners cumulatively produce loss-aware auditable Go record model through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Version record schema, semantic meaning, missingness, invariants, serialization, and migration separately from source markup.
Attach run, URL, response, node or attribute, raw digest, transform, timestamp, and decision evidence to each admitted field.
Preserve bounded causal rejected values and counts without leaking sensitive content or silently inventing defaults.

modules.title: Link-Bearing Elements, Relations, Base URLs, and Crawl Graph
modules.description: Only anchor tags are inspected, every href becomes a page, relation and base semantics disappear, and fetched URL sets stand in for a graph. Learners cumulatively produce semantic provenance-rich link graph through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Distinguish navigation, resource, metadata, embedded, form, media, and alternate URL-bearing HTML elements.
Use first valid base semantics against final response URL and retain accepted and rejected base evidence.
Interpret rel tokens, canonical, alternate, hreflang, download, media, nofollow, and target under explicit owner policy.

modules.title: Frontier Queues, Visited Reservation, State Transitions, and Determinism
modules.description: Workers share slices and maps, visited is marked after success, duplicate discoveries race, retries overwrite terminal state, and ordering changes every run. Learners cumulatively produce race-safe deterministic frontier state machine through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Define discovered, rejected, queued, claimed, fetching, fetched, parsed, retryable, terminal, and canceled states with legal transitions.
Reserve admitted URL keys atomically before enqueue so concurrent discoveries cannot duplicate effects.
Use stable priority and tie keys for owner commitments, depth, page class, freshness, origin fairness, and retry readiness.

modules.title: URL Space Traps, Structural Fingerprints, Soft Errors, and Stop Evidence
modules.description: Calendars, facets, search pages, sessions, sort orders, repeated path segments, and soft errors create limitless unique keys under the page cap. Learners cumulatively produce trap detection and bounded containment policy through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Detect temporal, pagination, facet, search, session, nonce, sort, repeated-segment, depth, and parameter-combination expansion.
Bound distinct path templates, query keys, values, combinations, depths, and pages per class with owned exceptions.
Combine status, redirect, title, main text, structure, known markers, and repetition to classify soft error and challenge pages.

modules.title: Per-Origin Politeness, Token Timing, Retry Budgets, and Fairness
modules.description: A shared ticker spaces starts globally, one origin monopolizes slots, Retry-After is ignored, jitter is unbounded, and transient failures retry forever. Learners cumulatively produce monotonic per-origin eligibility scheduler through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Track per-origin active requests, last start, next eligibility, request and byte budgets, robots state, and owner override.
Enforce minimum spacing between request starts with monotonic time and avoid holding shared locks while sleeping.
Classify transport and HTTP failures by method safety, response evidence, attempt, elapsed, and crawl budgets.

modules.title: Goroutine Pools, Context Causes, Backpressure, and Shutdown
modules.description: A goroutine is launched per URL, workers close shared channels, cancellation stops fetch but not parsing or writes, and main returns before cleanup. Learners cumulatively produce bounded leak-free Go crawl pipeline through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Bound frontier, workers, open bodies, parser memory, report buffers, files, and browser-service requests with end-to-end backpressure.
Assign channel creation, senders, receiver, closer, buffering rationale, and cancellation behavior without double close or blocked send.
Derive owner, run, item, attempt, deadline, and shutdown contexts and preserve the first meaningful cancellation cause.

modules.title: Colly Collectors, Callback Order, Limits, Storage, and Adoption Boundaries
modules.description: The code moves directly to Colly, mutates collector callbacks concurrently, enables Async without Wait, trusts IgnoreRobotsTxt defaults, and loses the hand-built policy model. Learners cumulatively produce policy-preserving Colly adapter and decision record through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Map Collector creation, callback registration, Visit, Request, Async, Wait, error, and close-related ownership into the application lifecycle.
Translate allowed domains, depth, body size, redirects, robots, rate limits, revisit, and user agent from owned policy and verify gaps.
Keep callbacks thin, order-independent where required, concurrency-safe, context-rich, and delegated to testable application services.

modules.title: Checkpoint Files, Atomic Replacement, Versioning, and Resume
modules.description: State is gob-encoded in place, partial writes look valid, policies drift, in-flight work becomes complete, and resuming can contact sites before review. Learners cumulatively produce crash-consistent policy-bound checkpoint through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Choose policy, seed, frontier, attempts, digests, records, reports, and counters that must survive versus recomputable state.
Use explicit portable schema, size limits, checksums, compatibility rules, and unknown-field policy rather than implicit struct layout.
Write temporary state, flush and sync under declared durability, validate, rename atomically, sync directory where required, and retain fallback.

modules.title: Content Digests, Near-Duplicate Evidence, Change Classes, and History
modules.description: Canonical URLs, byte hashes, text similarity, DOM structure, and latest records are collapsed into one destructive duplicate decision. Learners cumulatively produce multi-stage versioned content evidence store through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Label raw, decoded, DOM, selected-content, record, and rendered digests with algorithm and transformation version.
Distinguish URL alias, byte identity, admitted-record equality, replay, and observation duplication while retaining lineage.
Use token and structure features, thresholds, counterexamples, false-merge sampling, and stakeholder consequences.

modules.title: Indexing Directives, Canonical Graphs, Hreflang, and Structured Data
modules.description: The auditor trusts tags as commands, merges crawl and index policies, accepts malformed JSON-LD, and promises ranking or rich results from syntax. Learners cumulatively produce cross-signal search metadata report through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Compare titles and descriptions with visible purpose, uniqueness, language, changed versions, and stakeholder task without universal length claims.
Resolve canonical references and compare redirects, sitemap, internal links, content identity, reachability, and cycles.
Parse robots meta and X-Robots-Tag by product token, response, precedence, and scope while separating crawling from indexing.

modules.title: Static Accessibility Evidence, WCAG Mapping, and Review Handoff
modules.description: The crawler counts alt values and headings then declares WCAG compliance without computed names, interactions, contrast, reflow, or disabled-user review. Learners cumulatively produce WCAG-linked static finding ledger through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Classify informative, decorative, functional, linked, complex, grouped, and text images using surrounding purpose and review uncertainty.
Audit document language, headings, landmarks, labels, instructions, table relationships, and duplicate identifiers as evidence, not templates.
Identify native semantics and attribute risks while noting accessible names, roles, values, and states may need browser computation.

modules.title: Resource Graphs, Cache Evidence, Budgets, and Performance Non-Claims
modules.description: The tool reports document bytes as total page weight, ignores encoded versus decoded sizes and browser discovery, and turns one server timing into user performance. Learners cumulatively produce static resource and delivery evidence dossier through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Inventory document, style, script, image, font, media, module, preload, and third-party references with source and scope.
Separate wire, decoded, retained, intrinsic-media, rendered, and duplicate-transfer sizes with missing evidence visible.
Report cache fields, validators, variation, redirects, compression, and cold or warm assumptions without simulating browser cache blindly.

modules.title: Dynamic Rendering Decision and Isolated Browser-Service Boundary
modules.description: The Go crawler cannot satisfy one dynamic requirement, so it shells out to an uncontrolled browser process for every page and trusts returned HTML. Learners cumulatively produce least-authority dynamic-rendering service contract through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Require evidence that static retrieval cannot meet an authorized stakeholder need before admitting browser rendering.
Define typed request, origin policy, viewport, actions, readiness, budgets, response, errors, trace, and cancellation for a separate controlled service.
Apply authorization, SSRF, resource type, download, WebSocket, worker, and byte rules to every browser-initiated request.

modules.title: Authenticated Scope, Secrets, SSRF, Untrusted Content, and Privacy
modules.description: Credentials enter config files, cookies cross accounts, GET endpoints mutate state, extracted text reaches HTML and spreadsheets, and sensitive state persists in shared caches. Learners cumulatively produce least-authority secure crawl boundary through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Require explicit owner, account, role, purpose, paths, time, data, environment, and revocation authorization for sessions.
Acquire, scope, redact, rotate, destroy, and isolate secrets, cookies, CSRF material, caches, checkpoints, and reports per run and account.
Allow safe retrieval only, block state-changing forms, logout and destructive routes, and test unexpected GET side effects.

modules.title: Streaming JSON, CSV, Accessible HTML, and Remediation Workflows
modules.description: Outputs lack version and provenance, CSV formulas stay active, maps randomize order, giant tables fail reflow, and findings have no correction lifecycle. Learners cumulatively produce deterministic accessible report suite through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Version record envelopes, ordering, missingness, error rows, provenance, encoding, newline, and digest manifests for streaming JSON.
Define headers, ordering, delimiter, quoting, newline, Unicode, nulls, formula defense, row limits, and round-trip tests with encoding/csv.
Sort every externally visible map-derived set by stable typed keys and separate semantic order from serialization order.

modules.title: httptest, Custom RoundTrippers, Fuzzing, Race Evidence, and Mutations
modules.description: Tests use only friendly HTML and mocked functions, race tests replace logical invariants, fuzz targets accept everything, and no test catches a seeded defect. Learners cumulatively produce risk-layered native Go crawler test system through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Build original accepted, malformed, adversarial, Unicode, boundary, duplicate, inaccessible, privacy-sensitive, and changed fixtures with provenance.
Use custom RoundTrippers for request, redirect, body, cancellation, cleanup, and deterministic address decisions without network.
Use loopback HTTP and TLS servers for redirects, truncation, compression, encoding, slow headers and bodies, disconnects, retries, and shutdown.

modules.title: Telemetry, Capacity, Scheduling, Deployment, Backup, and Recovery
modules.description: Metrics use raw URLs, goroutine counts climb, cron overlaps jobs, binaries rebuild after approval, checkpoints are copied but never restored, and rollback contacts targets immediately. Learners cumulatively produce operable recoverable Go crawler service through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Correlate run, policy, URL key, attempt, response, parser, frontier, goroutine, report, and recovery while redacting content and bounding labels.
Measure CPU, heap, allocations, goroutines, open files, sockets, queue age, bytes, throughput, and origin fairness under representative fixtures.
Use one schedule owner, overlap lock, missed-run policy, configuration and artifact identity, owner pause, signal handling, and clean shutdown.

modules.title: Release Gates, Incident Drills, Rollback, and Residual-Risk Defense
modules.description: A successful demonstration becomes release approval despite open owner, parser, accessibility, capacity, recovery, and incident questions. Learners cumulatively produce Go crawler production defense dossier through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Require clean source-to-binary, module, unit, fuzz, race, mutation, loopback, accessibility, security, capacity, deployment, restore, and rollback gates.
Trace unfamiliar authorized seed through robots, scope, request, bytes, tree, record, graph, report, shutdown, checkpoint, and restored state with exact counts.
Rehearse owner revoke, runaway frontier, private redirect, credential leak, parser drift, inaccessible report, goroutine leak, corrupt checkpoint, and unsafe output.
